clue: The longest tennis match in history, between John Isner and Nicolas Mahut at Wimbledon 2010, ended with a final-set score of ___.
The last 'Timeless Test' cricket match in 1939 was abandoned as a draw after nine days of play because the England team had to ___.
The 1900 Paris Olympics featured live ___, the only time in Olympic history that animals were deliberately killed as part of an event.
Eddie Eagan is the only person ever to win Olympic gold at both the Summer and Winter Games in two different sports: boxing in 1920 and ___ in 1932.
The oldest Olympic medalist of all time is Swedish shooter Oscar Swahn, who won silver in 1920 at the age of ___.
Cricket's famous 'Ashes' trophy urn is reputed to contain the burnt remains of a ___.
Believe it or not, ___ was a full Olympic medal sport at every Summer Games from 1900 to 1920 before being dropped.
A regulation badminton shuttlecock is built from exactly ___ feathers, usually taken from a goose or duck.
At the Man versus Horse Marathon in Llanwrtyd Wells, Wales, it took years before a runner on foot finally beat every horse, claiming a jackpot that had grown to ___ pounds.
In the Eton Wall Game's showcase St Andrew's Day match, goals are so rare that the last one was scored in the year ___.
A chess boxing match alternates blitz chess with fistfights across a total of ___ rounds, starting and ending with chess.
The world altitude record for extreme ironing was set on Mount Everest at a height of ___ metres above sea level.
In sepak takraw, players volley a rattan ball over a net using their feet, knees, chest and head, but are strictly forbidden to use their ___.
The underwater sport now known as underwater hockey was invented in 1954 in Portsmouth under the original name ___.
In kabaddi, to prove they are not inhaling, the raider must continuously chant the word ___ throughout their entire raid on a single breath.
